---
title: "wh_excl_s1_da"
output: html_document
date: "2024-07-11"
---

```{r}
# Load necessary libraries for data manipulation and modeling
library(here)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(readr)
```

```{r}
# Read the CSV file into a dataframe
data <- read_csv("wh_excl_data_s1.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
```

```{r}
# Calculate summary statistics for each combination of mirativity, valence, and noteworthiness
summary_stats <- data %>%
  group_by(mirativity, valence, noteworthiness) %>%
  summarize(
    mean_selected = mean(felicity_rating, na.rm = TRUE),  # Calculate mean felicity rating
    sd_selected = sd(felicity_rating, na.rm = TRUE),  # Calculate standard deviation of felicity rating
    se_selected = sd_selected / sqrt(n()),  # Calculate standard error of the mean
    ci_lower = mean_selected - qt(0.975, df = n() - 1) * se_selected,  # Calculate lower bound of 95% CI
    ci_upper = mean_selected + qt(0.975, df = n() - 1) * se_selected,  # Calculate upper bound of 95% CI
    .groups = 'drop'  # Ungroup the data after summarizing
  )
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(summary_stats, aes(x = valence, y = mean_selected, fill = noteworthiness)) +
  # 1. Dodge bars and outline them
  geom_bar(
    stat = "identity",
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.9),
    color = "black",       # Thin black outline
    linewidth = 0.5        # Outline thickness
  ) +
  # 2. Add error bars
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(ymin = ci_lower, ymax = ci_upper),
    width = 0.2,
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.9)
  ) +
  # 3. Add individual data points with jitter + dodge
  geom_point(
    data = data,
    aes(x = valence, y = felicity_rating, fill = noteworthiness),
    position = position_jitterdodge(dodge.width = 0.9, jitter.width = 0.2),
    shape = 21,       # Allows separate fill and outline colors
    size = 2,
    stroke = 0.5,     # Outline thickness on the points
    alpha = 0.6,
    color = "black"   # Black outline around the points
  ) +
  # 4. Facet if needed
  facet_wrap(
    ~ mirativity,
    labeller = labeller(mirativity = c("+" = "+Mirativity", "-" = "-Mirativity"))
  ) +
  # 5. Labels and theme
  labs(
    x = "Valence",
    y = "Felicity Rating",
    fill = "Noteworthiness"    # legend title for fill
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    axis.text.x = element_text(hjust = 1),
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  ) +
  # 6. Optional: define custom color scale or y-axis limits
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("+" = "#F8A19F", "-" = "#86D4D3")) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 10), breaks = seq(0, 10, 2))

```

```{r}
# Fit a linear mixed-effects model with random slopes for valence within participants and random intercepts for items
best_model <- lmer(
  felicity_rating ~ mirativity * noteworthiness * valence + (1 + valence | submission_id) + (1 | itemname),
  data = data,
  REML = FALSE  # Use maximum likelihood estimation
)
```

```{r}
# Summarize the fitted model
summary(best_model)
```
